description: High, Medium, and Low all have trend='increasing' with positive changeCount. Tests that bold is applied to all 3 risk levels' names and changeCounts, and that all trend descriptions use increasing vocabulary.

High, Medium, and Low all have trend='increasing' with positive changeCount. Tests that bold is applied to all 3 risk levels' names and changeCounts, and that all trend descriptions use increasing vocabulary.
High, Medium, and Low all have trend='decreasing' with negative changeCount. Tests that no bolding is applied and that decreasing vocabulary is used for all 3 bullets.
High, Medium, and Low all have trend='decreasing' with negative changeCount. Tests that no bolding is applied and that decreasing vocabulary is used for all 3 bullets.
High has trend='increasing' (bold applied), Medium and Low have trend='decreasing' (no bold). This is the most realistic pattern. Tests selective bolding — only High bullet gets bold.
High has trend='increasing' (bold applied), Medium and Low have trend='decreasing' (no bold). This is the most realistic pattern. Tests selective bolding — only High bullet gets bold.
At least one risk level has trend='stable' (changeCount near 0) and another has trend='fluctuating'. Tests that stable/fluctuating vocabulary is used correctly and no bold is applied.
At least one risk level has trend='stable' (changeCount near 0) and another has trend='fluctuating'. Tests that stable/fluctuating vocabulary is used correctly and no bold is applied.
All counts > 1000 (requiring comma separators), monthlyData[] has only 2 entries (first and last month are the same as peakMonth/lowestMonth). Tests peakMonth omission rule and comma formatting.
All counts > 1000 (requiring comma separators), monthlyData[] has only 2 entries (first and last month are the same as peakMonth/lowestMonth). Tests peakMonth omission rule and comma formatting.
